findings.evidence: `rg` hit: `169:      (show Continuous (fun x : s => f x) from f.continuous.comp continuous_subtype_val)`.
findings.fix: Remove the `show` if elaboration accepts `f.continuous.comp continuous_subtype_val`, or add a short comment explaining the coercion mismatch being resolved.
findings.issue: `restrict` uses `show Continuous ... from ...` without documenting why the coercion goal needs this bridge.

findings.evidence: Lines 141-143 use nested `simpa`; Mathlib provides `BoundedContinuousFunction.coe_sub` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Topology/ContinuousMap/Bounded/Basic.lean:657`.
findings.fix: Reuse the already-defined negation closure and explicit coercion lemmas, e.g. prove from `f.memHolder.add (-g).memHolder` with `BoundedContinuousFunction.coe_sub`/`coe_neg`, or define subtraction through `f + -g`.
findings.issue: The `Sub` instance proof is a brittle `simpa [sub_eq_add_neg]` chain that depends on simplifying scalar multiplication by `(-1 : ℝ)` and bounded-function coercions.

summary: Proof-quality issues remain: one proof uses an undocumented `show`, and the subtraction closure proof relies on simplifier/defeq shape instead of the available coercion lemmas.
